element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns throttle, braking, steering on straightforward tracks
Default attack against nearby opponents; teaches stamina management
Holding directional button during attack; tactical positioning
Attack opponents wielding clubs to acquire them as temporary weapons
Cows, deer, cars, trees cause ejection; tests steering precision
Police apprehension after crashes; financial penalty; incentivizes clean racing
Cumulative crash damage; full depletion ends race and triggers repair cost
Visible UI feedback; depletion from combat causes ejection; teaches resource management
Between-race shop access; weight/speed/steering tradeoffs; progression gate

typical_tension_triggers: Approaching curves and obstacles at high speed
Low cash reserves threatening game-over state
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous tension with rhythmic peaks during opponent encounters and obstacle avoidance; relief comes at race completion and progression gates

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to racing mechanics and basic combat on Grass Valley and Sierra Nevada courses; player learns stamina and damage management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Addition of motor officers and weapon dynamics; Napa Valley introduces familiar but faster traffic patterns; players refine bike purchases and positioning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Pacific Coast Highway and final levels demand mastery of all systems: combat positioning, obstacle navigation, opponent stamina depletion, and financial management to avoid game-over via repair/fine penalties
notable_spikes: First motor officer encounter (introduces new fail condition)
Transition to Level 3+ (cumulative traffic density and opponent aggression)
Final races requiring specific bike stats that demand strategic purchasing

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must win 5 consecutive races on different Californian tracks within each of 5 levels; progression is linear and mandatory
Races cycle through distinct geographic settings (Grass Valley, Sierra Nevada, Napa Valley, Pacific Coast Highway) that repeat across levels with increasing difficulty
Early levels focus on basic punching; later levels introduce weapon pickups, aggressive opponent tactics, and police interference
Player begins with $1000; repair/fine penalties create escalating economic tension; bike upgrades become necessary but drain reserves
